advantages: Closed-form PMFs/PDFs and familiar parameters (p, n, λ, μ, σ, π) make models interpretable and fast to fit.
Conjugate and MLE stories are clean: sample proportions, means, and variances often suffice for classical estimators.
Direct correspondence to standard losses (cross-entropy, Poisson deviance, MSE) simplifies pipeline design from assumption to objective.
Central limit theorem justifies Normal approximations for sums and averages, enabling practical inference at scale.
disadvantages: Real data often violate assumptions: heavy tails, zero-inflation, over-dispersion, or multimodality break Poisson/Bernoulli/Gaussian simplicity.
Treating continuous measurements as exactly Gaussian ignores bounds, skew, and outliers that distort MLE and calibration.
Independence assumptions in Binomial/Poisson/Naive Bayes setups fail when trials or features are correlated.
Softmax/Categorical models with many rare classes need lots of data; uniform priors or imbalance can dominate predicted π_k.
